_notes: Fraud rings: (c1:Customer)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]->(c2:Customer) — CRITICAL: SHARED_IDENTIFIERS connects Customer to Customer (NOT Account to Account). Use MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-(c2:Customer) for ring detection.
Customer.fraudFlag is a STRING ('True' or 'False'), NOT a boolean — use WHERE c.fraudFlag = 'True' (not = true).
Each Customer has at most ONE email address (no customer has >1 USES_EMAIL link in current data).
Transaction.date is stored as DateTime — compare with t.date.year = 2025 or datetime() functions, NOT date() literals.
louvainCommunity (int) groups connected accounts into fraud communities.
pagerank and betweenness are float centrality scores — higher = more influential node.
SEARCH clause is available on local Neo4j 2026.02.1 (vector only); use for Chunk/embedding indexes.
Fulltext search: CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('customerNames', $query) YIELD node, score
SHORTEST N syntax is supported for finding shortest transaction paths.
QPE {m,n} syntax is supported for bounded fraud ring traversal.
